author: tuyen4818
content:
we still validate signatures of same-pubkey pending deposits before  onboard builders
so the frontrunning resistance is preserved
the change is actually very minimal, `is_pending_validator` already did that so we can just reuse it
create a Draft PR to make it clear https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5254
cc <@592004585506340885>

author: tuyen4818
content:
so if there are `n` same-pubkey validator deposits, we only need to validate that `n` signatures
validating the whole queue of pending deposits is not needed

author: jtraglia
content: Hey there. This is a smarter way of doing this. For the most part I like it. Let me give it a proper review in a bit.

author: jtraglia
content: Thanks again for the PR. I would like <@755590043632140352> to review this before it can be merged; he's good at catching issues in this function 😅 my only concern is that looping over pending deposits so many times is inefficient, but I expect clients will have a better way of dealing with this & it's still going to be faster than signature verifications.

author: jtraglia
content: If potuz & others approve of this change, I would like to include it in the upcoming release.

author: jtraglia
content: For convenience, this is what the function looks like now:

author: tuyen4818
content:
yeah clients will need to partition by pubkeys, even with the same pubkey we don't want to start validating from index 0 but the last validated index
this is just same to `is_pending_validator()`, I linked the lodestar implementation in the PR
we don't want to make the spec so complex for this

author: potuz
content: Oh you mean at the fork, not in the processing of requests. Yeah I think this PR is formally correct but it comes with other tradeoffs like if there's a single good signature for a validator and then 1k deposits for a builder with the same pubkey you'll verify the same signature 1k times instead of 1. It's too early for me on a bad night but let me think on this one for later today if possible

author: tuyen4818
content:
clients should not naively follow the spec, this situation is just the same to `is_pending_validator()`
on lodestar we verify pending validator once in that case and still follow the spec, need a separate cache for it

author: potuz
content: Yeah I agree, but the fact that just this exists and can potentially lead clients to do more work or need to add yet a new cache makes me unease. Anyway I think it's better than the current algorithm that anyway requires a cache or just die at the fork. Let me wake up and have some coffee before thinking more

author: tuyen4818
content:
<@845691100072509460> also made it a little cleaner https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5254#issuecomment-4458572983
<@592004585506340885> please coordinate and feel free to push to my branch if I'm not online

author: jtraglia
content: Oh that is even better. Pushed those changes with some small tweaks to the comments.

author: jtraglia
content: I should mention that we added a note about this when we introduced `is_pending_validator`.

author: potuz
content: Yeah I remember I asked to add that 🙂

author: potuz
content: One thing I like of this approach proposed now is that it may save funds that otherwise would be lost though

author: potuz
content: So I'm leaning towards approving it, need some time though, I'm clogged with bureaucracy now but I like the fact that invalid signatures could be saved with the new approach
